title: Viral titration by the Plaque assay
author: Elen Mello de Souza
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jqxcmxn

steps.instruction: Seed Vero lineage cells (kidney epithelial cells from African green monkey) in 24- well plates at a density of 2 x 10 5 cells/ well.

Maintain in RPMI 1640 medium (Cultilab) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (Cultilab), 2 mM L-glutamine (Gibco) and 100U/50µg penicillin/streptomycin (Sigma-Aldrich) for 24 h at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator.
Prepare serial ten-fold dilutions of viral suspension in sterile dilution tubes (10 -1 up to 10 -10 ).
Label 24-well plate containing confluent Vero cell monolayers for inoculation.
Add 100 µl of the viral suspension without dilution into each of the two wells of 24-well plate.
Add 100 µl of each serial dilution into each of the two wells of 24-well plate and maintain two wells without infection.
Incubate the inoculated plate at 37°C for 1 hour in a 5% CO2 incubator to allow for virus adsorption.
Add 1 ml of 3% semi-solid carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC) overlay medium to each well to limit the virus diffusion.
Maintain the plate for seven days at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator.
Incubate cell monolayer with 1 ml of the 10% formol for a minimum 18 hours at room temperature for fixation.
Remove the overlay medium and fixer.
Wash cell monolayer in running water (3 times).
Stain cell monolayer with 1 ml of the 0.04% Violet crystal for 1 hour at room temperature.
Count viral plaques for calculate plaque-forming unit (PFU).
